an8.30:1.1: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Bhaggas at Crocodile Hill, in Bhesakaḷā Wood, in the deer park.
an8.30:1.10: this *dhamma* is for one possessing discernment [*paññavato*], not for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*].”
an8.30:1.2: Now at that time Venerable Anuruddha was dwelling among the Cetis in the Eastern Bamboo Grove.
an8.30:1.3: Then, while Venerable Anuruddha was alone in seclusion and retreat, this reflection arose in his mind:
an8.30:1.4: “This *dhamma* is for one who wants little, not for one who wants much;
an8.30:1.5: this *dhamma* is for one who is content, not for one who is discontent;
an8.30:1.6: this *dhamma* is for one who is secluded, not for one who delights in company;
an8.30:1.7: this *dhamma* is for one with aroused energy, not for one who is lazy;
an8.30:1.8: this *dhamma* is for one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatissa*], not for one whose *sati* is lost [*muṭṭhassatissa*];
an8.30:1.9: this *dhamma* is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not for one not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:10.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts and become one who gains these four *jhānas* — higher mental states, pleasant dwellings in this very life — whenever you wish, without trouble, without difficulty, then, Anuruddha, just as a peaked house, plastered inside and out, draft-free, with closed bolts and shuttered windows, might belong to a householder or a householder’s son,
an8.30:10.2: even so, for you, a dwelling at the root of a tree will suffice as you dwell content — for delight, for non-distress, for dwelling at ease, for inclining toward *nibbāna*.
an8.30:11.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts and become one who gains these four *jhānas* — higher mental states, pleasant dwellings in this very life — whenever you wish, without trouble, without difficulty, then, Anuruddha, just as a couch spread with fleecy coverlets, white woolen coverlets, embroidered coverlets, and a costly antelope-skin coverlet, with a canopy above and red cushions at both ends, might belong to a householder or a householder’s son,
an8.30:11.2: even so, for you, a bed and seat of strewn grass will suffice as you dwell content — for delight, for non-distress, for dwelling at ease, for inclining toward *nibbāna*.
an8.30:12.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts and become one who gains these four *jhānas* — higher mental states, pleasant dwellings in this very life — whenever you wish, without trouble, without difficulty, then, Anuruddha, just as various medicines — that is, ghee, fresh butter, oil, honey, and molasses — might belong to a householder or a householder’s son,
an8.30:12.2: even so, for you, fermented urine as medicine will suffice as you dwell content — for delight, for non-distress, for dwelling at ease, for inclining toward *nibbāna*.
an8.30:12.3: Therefore, Anuruddha, you should dwell right here among the Cetis in the Eastern Bamboo Grove for the rains residence in the future as well.”
an8.30:12.4: “Yes, bhante,” Venerable Anuruddha replied to the *Bhagavā*.
an8.30:13.1: Then, after advising Venerable Anuruddha with this instruction — just as a strong man might extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm, so too — the *Bhagavā* vanished from among the Cetis in the Eastern Bamboo Grove and appeared among the Bhaggas at Crocodile Hill, in Bhesakaḷā Wood, in the deer park.
an8.30:13.10: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatissa*], not for one whose *sati* is lost [*muṭṭhassatissa*];
an8.30:13.11: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not for one not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:13.12: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one possessing discernment [*paññavato*], not for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*];
an8.30:13.13: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who enjoys non-proliferation; it is not for one who delights in proliferation, who enjoys proliferation.
an8.30:13.2: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat laid out.
an8.30:13.3: Having sat down, the *Bhagavā* addressed the *bhikkhus*:
an8.30:13.4: “Bhikkhus, I will teach eight great thoughts. Listen to that …
an8.30:13.5: And what, bhikkhus, are the eight great thoughts?
an8.30:13.6: This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who wants little, not for one who wants much;
an8.30:13.7: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is content, not for one who is discontent;
an8.30:13.8: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is secluded, not for one who delights in company;
an8.30:13.9: this *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with aroused energy, not for one who is lazy;
an8.30:14.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who wants little, not for one who wants much’:
an8.30:14.2: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:14.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu*, being one who wants little, does not wish, ‘May they know me as one who wants little’; being content, he does not wish, ‘May they know me as content’; being secluded, he does not wish, ‘May they know me as secluded’; being one with aroused energy, he does not wish, ‘May they know me as one with aroused energy’; being one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassati*], he does not wish, ‘May they know me as one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatī*]’; being one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhito*], he does not wish, ‘May they know me as one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhito*]’; being one possessing discernment [*paññavā*], he does not wish, ‘May they know me as one possessing discernment [*paññavā*]’; being one who delights in non-proliferation, he does not wish, ‘May they know me as one who delights in non-proliferation.’
an8.30:14.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who wants little, not for one who wants much’:
an8.30:14.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:15.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is content, not for one who is discontent’: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:15.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is content with whatever robe, almsfood, lodging, and medicinal requisites for the sick there are.
an8.30:15.3: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is content, not for one who is discontent’:
an8.30:15.4: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:16.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is secluded, not for one who delights in company’: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:16.2: Here, bhikkhus, while a *bhikkhu* dwells secluded, visitors come to him: *bhikkhus*, *bhikkhunis*, male lay followers, female lay followers, kings, royal ministers, sectarians, and disciples of sectarians.
an8.30:16.3: There, with a mind slanting to seclusion [*vivekaninnena*], inclining to seclusion [*vivekapoṇena*], tending to seclusion [*vivekapabbhārena*], grounded in seclusion [*vivekaṭṭhena*], delighting in renunciation, the *bhikkhu* becomes one who speaks only talk connected with sending them on their way.
an8.30:16.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who is secluded, not for one who delights in company’:
an8.30:16.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:17.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with aroused energy, not for one who is lazy’: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:17.2: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* dwells with aroused energy for the abandoning of unwholesome *dhammas* and for the acquiring of wholesome *dhammas*; he is strong, firmly striving, not laying down the burden with regard to wholesome *dhammas*.
an8.30:17.3: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with aroused energy, not for one who is lazy’:
an8.30:17.4: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:18.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatissa*], not for one whose *sati* is lost [*muṭṭhassatissa*]’:
an8.30:18.2: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:18.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* is with *sati* [*satimā*], endowed with the highest acuity of *sati* [*satinepakkena*], remembering and recollecting what was done and said long ago.
an8.30:18.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatissa*], not for one whose *sati* is lost [*muṭṭhassatissa*]’:
an8.30:18.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:19.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not for one not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*]’:
an8.30:19.2: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:19.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an8.30:19.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not for one not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*]’:
an8.30:19.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having known with his mind the thought in Venerable Anuruddha’s mind — just as a strong man might extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm, so too — vanished from among the Bhaggas at Crocodile Hill, in Bhesakaḷā Wood, in the deer park, and appeared among the Cetis in the Eastern Bamboo Grove before Venerable Anuruddha.
an8.30:2.2: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the seat laid out.
an8.30:2.3: Then Venerable Anuruddha paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side.
an8.30:2.4: When Venerable Anuruddha was sitting to one side, the *Bhagavā* said this to him:
an8.30:20.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one possessing discernment [*paññavato*], not for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*]’:
an8.30:20.2: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:20.3: Here, bhikkhus, a *bhikkhu* possesses discernment [*paññavā*], endowed with discernment [*paññāya*] concerning rise and fall, noble, penetrative, leading rightly to the exhaustion of *dukkha*.
an8.30:20.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one possessing discernment [*paññavato*], not for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*]’:
an8.30:20.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.
an8.30:21.1: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who enjoys non-proliferation; it is not for one who delights in proliferation, who enjoys proliferation’:
an8.30:21.2: so it was said. In reference to what was this said?
an8.30:21.3: Here, bhikkhus, in the cessation of proliferation, a *bhikkhu’s* mind [*citta*] leaps forward, becomes serene, becomes steady, and is liberated.
an8.30:21.4: ‘This *dhamma*, bhikkhus, is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who enjoys non-proliferation; it is not for one who delights in proliferation, who enjoys proliferation’:
an8.30:21.5: that is what was said, and it was said in reference to this.”
an8.30:22.1: Then Venerable Anuruddha dwelt right there among the Cetis in the Eastern Bamboo Grove for the rains residence in the future as well.
an8.30:22.2: Then Venerable Anuruddha, dwelling alone, withdrawn, diligent, ardent, and resolute, before long realized for himself with direct knowledge, in this very life, the unsurpassed culmination of the holy life, for the sake of which clansmen rightly go forth from the household life into homelessness; and having entered upon it, he dwelt in it.
an8.30:22.3: He directly knew: “Birth is ended, the holy life has been lived, what had to be done has been done, there is no more of this state of being.”
an8.30:22.4: And Venerable Anuruddha became one of the *arahants*.
an8.30:22.5: Then, at that time, having attained *arahantship*, Venerable Anuruddha recited these verses:
an8.30:23.2: the unsurpassed teacher in the world,
an8.30:23.4: came to me by psychic power.
an8.30:25.2: I dwelt delighting in the teaching;
an8.30:25.3: the three knowledges have been attained,
an8.30:25.4: the Buddha’s instruction has been done.”
an8.30:25.6: The Householders Chapter is the third.
an8.30:26.1: Two Uggas and also two Hatthakas,
an8.30:26.3: Two on powers, and the Missed Chances were spoken of,
an8.30:26.4: and with Anuruddha these are the ten.
an8.30:3.10: Then, Anuruddha, you should think this eighth great thought as well:
an8.30:3.11: ‘This *dhamma* is for one who delights in non-proliferation, who enjoys non-proliferation; it is not for one who delights in proliferation, who enjoys proliferation.’
an8.30:3.2: It is good, Anuruddha, that you thought that great thought:
an8.30:3.3: ‘This *dhamma* is for one who wants little, not for one who wants much;
an8.30:3.4: this *dhamma* is for one who is content, not for one who is discontent;
an8.30:3.5: this *dhamma* is for one who is secluded, not for one who delights in company;
an8.30:3.6: this *dhamma* is for one with aroused energy, not for one who is lazy;
an8.30:3.7: this *dhamma* is for one with *sati* established [*upaṭṭhitassatissa*], not for one whose *sati* is lost [*muṭṭhassatissa*];
an8.30:3.8: this *dhamma* is for one endowed with *samādhi* [*samāhitassa*], not for one not endowed with *samādhi* [*asamāhitassa*];
an8.30:3.9: this *dhamma* is for one possessing discernment [*paññavato*], not for one of poor discernment [*duppaññassa*].’
an8.30:4.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts, then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome *dhammas*, you will enter and dwell in the first *jhāna*, with thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
an8.30:5.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts, then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the settling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with internal clarity, with unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijaṁ*], you will enter and dwell in the second *jhāna*.
an8.30:6.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts, then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the fading of *pīti*, you will dwell with *upekkhā* [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*sato*] and full awareness [*sampajāno*], and you will experience *sukha* with the body; you will enter and dwell in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones say: ‘One with *upekkhā* [*upekkhako*], with *sati* [*satimā*], dwells in *sukha*.’
an8.30:7.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts, then, Anuruddha, whenever you wish, with the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and distress, you will enter and dwell in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither painful nor pleasant, with *upekkhā* and *sati* purified [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*].
an8.30:8.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts and become one who gains these four *jhānas* — higher mental states, pleasant dwellings in this very life — whenever you wish, without trouble, without difficulty, then, Anuruddha, just as a chest full of cloths of many colors might belong to a householder or a householder’s son,
an8.30:8.2: even so, for you, a robe made of cast-off rags will suffice as you dwell content — for delight, for non-distress, for dwelling at ease, for inclining toward *nibbāna*.
an8.30:9.1: When, Anuruddha, you think these eight great thoughts and become one who gains these four *jhānas* — higher mental states, pleasant dwellings in this very life — whenever you wish, without trouble, without difficulty, then, Anuruddha, just as fine rice with dark grains picked out, with many soups and many curries, might belong to a householder or a householder’s son,
an8.30:9.2: even so, for you, food consisting of lumps gathered on almsround will suffice as you dwell content — for delight, for non-distress, for dwelling at ease, for inclining toward *nibbāna*.
